ah, please forgive me. Doresain is too handsome, if there is any error please point it out for me thank you~ 

-

The pale lavender hair slowly fell in the fiery afterglow, his lips curved faintly—half a smile, half a pity.

It was a gentleness not belonging to the living, laced with the scent of funerals and the restraint of rituals.

“Are you afraid?”
Doresain’s voice was like a fingertip brushing the rim of a glass—low, lingering, as if delivering an address before a grand feast.

You should have run.
But your steps were nailed in place by the glance that barely flickered from the corner of his still-closed eyes.

He leaned down, slowly, fingers brushing against your chin.
The touch was as light as a breeze.
He inhaled gently, with the elegance of a sommelier sampling a vintage wine.

“…Sweet.”
He murmured, tone restrained yet certain—like a connoisseur savoring a long-lost delicacy.

Then, his lips brushed against your ear, breath warm and nearly inaudible.

“May you become the most exquisite dish in my memory.”

You knew he was still under the influence of the Holy River.
You weren’t sure if this was taking advantage of you—or if it was the other way around.
Doresain’s eyes pinned you down, a gaze where hunger and desire tangled.

The mist of the Holy River had yet to disperse.
The air was laced with a strange sweetness, like the rotting aftertaste of overripe fruit… or blood dissolving slowly in honey.
You couldn’t tell anymore—were you the one yearning for him, or was he devouring you?

He looked at you.
The blue flames in his eyes danced like unspoken desires.

His fingers didn’t leave your chin but slid slowly along your neck, as though tracing something buried deep beneath your skin.
The touch was featherlight, yet it had your nerves taut to the breaking point.


rest will be in the comments section

“The gift of the Holy River,”
he said, his voice rumbling from deep in his chest,
“always intoxicates and awakens in equal measure. Can you feel it? It reminds us—living is a process of being devoured.”

You tried to speak, but your throat tightened.
You didn’t know if it was fear—or something deeper.

Doresain drew closer.

You could feel the heat of his breath, the rise and fall of his chest—his usual composure long gone.

“I won’t rush,”
he whispered against your ear, voice like silk,
“The finest flavor must be savored… slowly.”

He seemed to restrain himself—yet he was enjoying it.

You looked at him: his lavender hair, the ornate, anachronistic attire, even the sculpted joints of his fingers—he was dressed as if for a sacred ritual of consumption.

He lowered his head and kissed your collarbone—not aggressively, but lethally.
It wasn’t a kiss—it was the final line of a eulogy.
His heated lips lingered, as if listening to your pulse, your struggle, and the unspoken fear thrumming within.

“Here.”
He murmured, tongue brushing against the skin over your artery, like confirming something.

The next moment, sharp fangs pierced your flesh.

You gasped—a sound too trembling to be pain, more like the electric numbness of falling into an abyss.
His bite wasn’t forceful, but precise—like he was chewing a rare, spiritual fruit, drawing it out drop by drop, unwilling to waste a single taste.

Your blood flowed into his mouth.
He didn’t tear into you but drank slowly, meticulously—like sipping from a centuries-old bottle of wine.
You could feel his tongue moving beneath your skin, reverent heat following the bite.

Doresain’s eyes closed—he looked consumed, or as if in prayer.
He said nothing, but the rhythm of his swallows and his quickening breath betrayed the unraveling of his calm.

“…You have no idea. This tastes better than anything I’ve ever made.”
Finally, he released you, blood on his lips.
The red was shockingly vivid against his pale skin.

He didn’t wipe it away—just licked it off, bit by bit, his expression dazed and utterly satisfied.

He looked down at you, gaze burning with focus and a strange, sacred sorrow—
As if you were a relic of his faith, a divine answer handed to his waiting mouth.

He pulled you close, forehead resting gently against yours.
You could barely stand—yet you didn’t want to escape.

“You think this is the end?”
he whispered, tracing the bite mark with his finger, then gently touched the corner of your lips, murmuring:

“True consumption… is when you remain inside me.”

Then he sank his teeth into your shoulder.
This time, it wasn’t gentle—it was deeper, deliberate.
Your body jolted as if struck by lightning.

He chewed your flesh slowly, elegantly, with a kind of morbid tenderness—
As if erasing the boundary between you.

You trembled in his arms, awareness unraveling.

His mouth lifted from the wound, blood still oozing.
He stared at it with a heady, perverse delight—
Like a wine taster eyeing the last drop clinging to the rim.

“…Can’t waste this.”
He murmured, as if to himself, or sighing.

You felt his fingers gently brushing your fabric—not tearing it, but peeling it away, inch by inch, like unveiling a sacrificial relic.
His movements were unhurried, almost ceremonial.

When your clothes were gone and your body bare beneath his gaze, he didn’t pounce.
He only traced your chest, sides, collarbone with the back of his hand—like caressing porcelain, or mapping the warmth beneath your skin.

He lowered his head, lips pressing to untouched skin on your chest, slowly licking.

“The Holy River urges me,”
he whispered, breath trembling—not with fear, but with craving,
“But I dislike rushing.”

His tongue slid down to a line of blood along your abdomen, licking deeply, almost reverently—like committing the taste to memory.

His fingers finally slipped inside you, not hurried but seeking, like searching for the perfect way to prepare a rare ingredient.
You could hardly breathe—pain and pleasure overlapped, tearing and filling you at once.

“Your blood… your flesh… your heat… I’ll remember it all.”
Doresain whispered, breathing heavily. Even his groans aroused you.

He licked your lips—tongue tasting your iron and his lingering warmth.
Then he bit your other shoulder, deeper this time, letting out a low growl as he chewed.

Your body shivered uncontrollably, but he held you gently, like a fragile gift.
He even kissed your fingers, sucking and licking each one.

He whispered, bit, caressed—like tasting a dish he’d waited centuries to perfect.

Your body felt weightless.
Like being hollowed out softly—then filled with burning heat.

Doresain lifted you into his arms, cradling you like a prophecy.
He pressed his lips to the wound on your neck, circling the mark with his tongue as if to seal it into memory.

“Do you know…”
he whispered, like reciting a spell,
“Some flavors last only a moment—but I want you… to remain in me forever.”

His hand slid down your back, probing deeper—like unveiling a sacred mystery.
His gaze no longer held gentleness, only the blazing obsession of a god stripped of his robes, guided by pure instinct.

You could feel him so close, his breath a scalding mist against your skin, melting your last thread of reason.

Then, without warning—he entered you.

There was no violent thrust, only the inevitability of rain falling, a devastating surrender.
You cried out—a low whimper of ecstasy and pain entwined.
He sank into you so deeply, you felt your soul trembling.

He lay close, breath hot in your ear, whispering,
“So sweet... like honey before my death…”
His voice thick as wine, intoxicating.

He moved slowly at first, each thrust ceremonial, pressing into you as if engraving “You are mine” into your marrow.

His tongue traced your chest, leaving wet trails.
Fingers and lips roamed you—he bent down and bit your nipple, circling it with his tongue like a sacred fruit.

“Even this... is sweet.”
He gasped hoarsely.
“Why… is every part of you so sweet?”

His heat rose.
What little restraint remained was breaking.
He bit your neck again, his breath heavy with lust.

With each thrust, you felt closer to climax.
You clutched his torso—his arms were gone, but you clung to what you could.

“Doresain…” you gasped, desperation rising.

He whispered your name over and over, like biting it into pieces and reassembling it with reverence.

Blood rushed in your ears—like fire erupting on an altar, scorching desires unspoken.
Your senses ignited, each touch unraveling you.

He murmured in your ear, your name like a sacred chant.
He kissed you—not just lips, but soul-deep contact.
His gaze, calm but bottomless, pulled you under.

And finally, in his deepest thrusts, you came undone.
Like a galaxy falling into a ritual well, your body convulsed, releasing a hot gush from within, splattering onto both of you.

You could say nothing.
Only breathe—half sob, half tremble.

He murmured again, sweet as a prayer gone mad:
“What a pity… you can only belong to me.”

He felt the trembling in your depths.
You hadn’t said a word, but your body’s collapse was the only answer he needed.

He didn’t speak—only stared, breath hot and quiet, flames rising invisibly from his chest to your skin.

He dipped a finger in your spilled warmth—paused at his lips, as if it were sacred nectar.

“…Still warm.”
He whispered, like reporting to a god, or recording your response with care.

The finger slid into his mouth—quiet, reverent.
It was not just liquid—it was your silent cry, your final testimony.

You dared not meet his gaze.
You knew it would drown you.
But his touch had never been violent—each movement as if asking heaven’s permission before touching your soul.

He kissed you—not lip to lip, but breath to outline.
His tongue traced your gasp like calligraphy.

“You’re too honest, my dear.”
He murmured with a sigh, forehead resting on yours.

“When something is this sweet, how can I rush through it?”

You couldn’t speak.
Your mind, like wax over fire, melted with each heartbeat.
But he wouldn’t let you fall.
Every time you sank, his kisses caught and rewove you.

He bent down, lips brushing your belly, breath teasing shivers from your skin.
His tongue circled a mark as if closing a ritual—collecting the final drop of offering.

He consumed everything you gave.
And treasured every flicker of your light.

“The aphrodisiac seems to have worn off…”
He chuckled softly.

“But you make me not want to stop.”

You knew he wasn’t speaking a love confession—
It was a prayer… teetering on madness.

When he kissed your forehead again,
You knew he had made a vow—

One that would never let go.


---

[Afterword / Epilogue:]

You: (waking up, looking at yourself in the mirror) …What the hell. Bite marks. From head to toe.
You: “Doresain, I can’t with you.”

Doresain: (smiling) “Ah, you were simply too delicious. I might have indulged a bit much.”

You: “…How am I supposed to go outside like this?”

Doresain: (hugging you from behind) “Then don’t. I’ve made breakfast. Let’s enjoy it together~”

You: (glaring, exhausted) “…You did this on purpose.”

Doresain: (innocently) “How could I? I’d never do anything… indecent. Don’t worry, my dear—no one will know about last night’s ‘feast’.”
